body:
## Summary

Solves #47 ("compare all competitors in all scopes ... make sure we have the richest features set") by delivering the requested case study under [`docs/case-studies/issue-47/`](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/blob/issue-47-76af108c0f24/docs/case-studies/issue-47/README.md) and decomposing the work into 15 filed GitHub sub-issues with native blocked-by ordering.

Fixes #47

## What is in this PR (docs-only)

| Document | Contents |
|---|---|
| [`README.md`](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/blob/issue-47-76af108c0f24/docs/case-studies/issue-47/README.md) | Case-study index, key findings, requirements → deliverables map, filed sub-issue table |
| [`requirements.md`](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/blob/issue-47-76af108c0f24/docs/case-studies/issue-47/requirements.md) | Traceable register of all 22 requirements (R-1 ... R-22) extracted from issue #47, each with current-state evidence (`file:symbol`): 2 Implemented, 15 Partial, 5 Missing |
| [`competitors-code-tooling.md`](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/blob/issue-47-76af108c0f24/docs/case-studies/issue-47/competitors-code-tooling.md) | 22-project survey (tree-sitter, Roslyn, ast-grep, Semgrep, Comby, Babel/Recast, Stratego, Rascal, srcML, difftastic, ...) incl. test-suite locations for corpus porting |
| [`competitors-natural-language.md`](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/blob/issue-47-76af108c0f24/docs/case-studies/issue-47/competitors-natural-language.md) | Natural-language grammar systems (GF+RGL, UD, LanguageTool/nlprule) and concept spaces (Wikidata lexemes, WordNet CILI; BabelNet rejected on licensing) |
| [`formats-storage-apis.md`](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/blob/issue-47-76af108c0f24/docs/case-studies/issue-47/formats-storage-apis.md) | Data-exchange format grammars compatible with tree-sitter 0.25.8, doublets-rs/links-notation/link-cli/formal-ai storage targets, Rust API-style precedents |
| [`solution-plans.md`](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/blob/issue-47-76af108c0f24/docs/case-studies/issue-47/solution-plans.md) | One plan per requirement cluster (S-1 ... S-15) with library reuse decisions and 4-phase dependency ordering |
| [`proposed-issues/`](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/tree/issue-47-76af108c0f24/docs/case-studies/issue-47/proposed-issues) | The 15 source specs + idempotent `create-issues.sh` filing script (issue #3 precedent) |
| [`raw-data/`](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/tree/issue-47-76af108c0f24/docs/case-studies/issue-47/raw-data) | Raw GitHub snapshots of issue #47, its comments, PR #48, and all repo issues |

## Filed sub-issues (#49–#63)

All 15 specs were filed, attached as sub-issues of #47 via the `sub_issues` API, and blocked-by relations wired via the native issue-dependencies API:

- Phase 1 (parallel): #49 readonly/mutable engine, #50 format grammars, #51 programming-language grammar wave, #52 pluggable parser registry, #53 LiNo network serialization, #56 natural-language grammar parsing, #57 exact-match shared concept space, #60 query/transform algebra, #61 incremental re-parse + structural sharing
- Phase 2: #54 doublets binary storage (blocked by #49, #53), #55 Rust types↔links codec (#53), #58 translation-rule registry (#52, #57)
- Phase 3: #59 language restriction profiles (#58), #62 API-style parity contract (#54, #58)
- Phase 4 (closing gate): #63 competitor corpora wave 2 + coverage ratchet (#50, #56, #60, #62)

Per issue #47's instruction, every sub-issue states that its work lands on this branch (`issue-47-76af108c0f24`) and merges through this PR.

## How to verify

- `gh api repos/link-foundation/meta-language/issues/47 --jq .sub_issues_summary` → `{"total":15,...}`
- `gh api repos/link-foundation/meta-language/issues/63/dependencies/blocked_by --jq '[.[].number]'` → `[50,56,60,62]`
- Docs-only change set: no code paths touched; changelog fragment not required for `./docs` markdown per `release.yml`.

Issue: https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/issues/50

Add an AccessMode { Mutable, ReadOnly } setting to ParseConfiguration
(default Mutable, no behavior change for existing callers) and a frozen
read-only engine boundary.

- ReadOnlyNetwork: a compile-time read-only view that derefs to
  &LinkNetwork, exposing every non-mutating operation while leaving the
  &mut self mutators unreachable (no DerefMut). Held behind an Arc so
  clones share one allocation.
- LinkNetwork::freeze / as_read_only produce the view; EngineNetwork and
  LinkNetwork::parse_engine honour the configured access mode and reject
  mutation at runtime with a ReadOnlyViolation diagnostic.
- Snapshot interop: NetworkSnapshot::as_read_only / from_read_only reuse
  the snapshot's Arc<LinkNetwork>, composing with snapshot versioning
  instead of duplicating it.
- Tests cover freeze -> query/reconstruct/verify, compile-fail mutation
  (doctest), runtime ReadOnlyViolation, snapshot interop, and Arc sharing.
- Changelog fragment (bump: minor); README usage example.

…, protobuf, GraphQL)

Wire seven tree-sitter grammar crates through src/tree_sitter_adapter.rs
following the PR #44-#46 pattern: tree-sitter-json, tree-sitter-yaml,
tree-sitter-toml-ng, tree-sitter-xml (XML + DTD), tree-sitter-ini,
tree-sitter-proto, and tree-sitter-graphql. Each format parses into real
LinkType::Syntax concrete-syntax links and reconstructs byte-for-byte.

- Add DATA_FORMAT_TARGETS registry and LanguageFamily::DataFormat in
  src/parity.rs with parity-gate tests mirroring MARKUP_LANGUAGE_TARGETS.
- Add per-format UTF-8 LANGUAGE_FIXTURES round-trip entries, alias coverage,
  a JSON recovery case, and a JSON-in-Markdown-fence mixed-region test.
- Document the seven grammars and the explicit CSV/JSON5 deferral
  (both still pin tree-sitter ~0.20) in docs/parity-roadmap.md.

Closes #50

Issue: https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/issues/53

Introduce ParserRegistry mapping language keys to Arc<dyn LanguageParser>
with the built-in parser as a fallback. User registrations shadow the
built-in dispatch for the same (case-insensitive) key; unregistered keys
still route through the built-in set.

- LinkNetwork::parse_with_registry dispatches a parse through a registry
- examples/custom_parser_registry.rs documents registering a custom parser
- Unit tests cover new-key registration, shadowing, case-insensitivity,
  and unchanged default behavior

Closes #52
commits.messageHeadline: Add pluggable ParserRegistry for user-registrable grammars

commits.messageBody:
…lin, Scala, Lua)

Wire five tree-sitter grammar crates through src/tree_sitter_adapter.rs
following the data-exchange-format pattern: tree-sitter-php (LANGUAGE_PHP),
tree-sitter-swift, tree-sitter-kotlin-ng (kotlin/kt), tree-sitter-scala, and
tree-sitter-lua. Each parses into real LinkType::Syntax concrete-syntax links
and reconstructs byte-for-byte.

- Add SECOND_TIER_PROGRAMMING_LANGUAGE_TARGETS registry in src/parity.rs with a
  parity-gate test mirroring DATA_FORMAT_TARGETS.
- Add per-language UTF-8 LANGUAGE_FIXTURES round-trip entries, case-insensitive
  alias coverage, and per-language recovery fixtures with error/missing
  diagnostics in tests/unit/grammar_parsing.rs.
- Pin scala 0.25.1 and lua 0.2.0 so the emitted parser ABI loads under the
  project's tree-sitter 0.25.x runtime; all five crates use the
  tree-sitter-language ABI binding (tree-sitter is a dev-dependency only).
- Document the five wired grammars and the explicit Perl deferral (its only
  binding pins tree-sitter ^0.26.3 as a normal dependency, tracked in #70) in
  docs/parity-roadmap.md.

Closes #51

commits.messageBody: Pluggable language-parser registry (user-registrable grammars and parser dispatch)

Align serialized output with the wider links-notation ecosystem and let
tests feed to_lino output back through the crate parser.

Make LinkId/Link/LinkNetwork fields pub(crate) so the serialization
module can construct and read links directly, keeping link_network.rs
under the file-size limit.
commits.messageHeadline: Expose LinkNetwork internals to sibling modules as pub(crate)

commits.messageBody:
Serialize the whole network to canonical links-notation text keyed by
each link's numeric id (doublets-style discipline), covering references,
names, types, terms, definitions, languages, spans, parse flags, and
term registration; reconstruct the exact network back. String payloads
are percent-encoded into single escape-free tokens. Adds
LinoSerializationError.

Add ParityCapability::LinoSerialization to the links-notation target and
a whole-network serialization parity fixture so coverage includes the
output (serialization) side, not just parsing.
commits.messageHeadline: Add LinoSerialization parity capability and output-side fixture

commits.messageBody:
Add round-trip property tests over every language fixture plus synthetic
networks, assert to_lino output is accepted by the links-notation crate
parser, exercise the LinoSerialization parity fixture, and add a
bump: minor changelog fragment.
commits.messageHeadline: Test to_lino/from_lino round-trip and crate-parser acceptance

commits.messageBody: Wire the next programming-language grammar wave (PHP, Swift, Kotlin, Scala, Lua; Perl deferred)
